compiled_state.title: validate.sh: CI gate caches stale failure baseline — reports FAIL after CI recovers
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Problem

The CI gate in validate.sh caches a ci-baseline file in /tmp/ that records the most recent CI status. Once a failure is cached, subsequent runs continue reporting ci: FAIL even after CI has recovered. The baseline file gets re-created from the old failure run during each validate.sh invocation.

## Expected Behavior

CI gate should reflect current CI status, not a cached historical failure.

## Actual Behavior

ci: FAIL persists across validation runs even after 4+ consecutive passing CI runs.

## Severity

P3 — transient state issue; causes confusion during /dso:debug-everything sessions.

comments.body: Investigated: ci-baseline is written but NEVER read back by validate.sh — it is a red herring. The real mechanism is check_ci()'s 'pending + prev_failure → immediate FAIL' logic which uses live GitHub data, not cached state. This is conservative-correct behavior: when the last completed run failed and a new run is still pending, reporting FAIL is appropriate. The ci-baseline file IS read by ci-status.sh's check_regression() for --wait flows, but that's separate from validate.sh's FAIL/PASS outcome. No code fix needed.
comments.author: Test
